# [PE_297](../PE_297.java)

With Fibonacci numbers up to $F_n$, the biggest sum of nonconsecutive terms is made with $F_x + F_{x-2} + F_{x-4} + \ldots + F_{b}$ where 
$$b = \begin{cases} 2 & \text{if}\ x \equiv 0 \pmod{2} \\ 1 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$$  
This sum is always less than  
$$F_{x+1} =$$
$$= F_{x} + F_{x-1} =$$
$$= F_{x} + F_{x-2} + F_{x-3} =$$
$$F_{x} + F_{x-2} + F_{x-4} + \ldots + F_{b} + 1$$
Therefore the Zeckendorf representation of $n$ must include the largest $F_{x}$ that is not larger than $n$. Take for example $100$. Taking away $F_{10} = 89$ gives $11$. Now we need to get the Zeckendorf representation of $11$. Taking away $F_{5} = 8$ gives $3$, which is itself a Fibonacci number $F_{3}$. If we start with a Fibonacci number, e.g. $F_{7} = 21$, $21$ itself is the one-element Zeckendorf representation.

Using this, we can make some deductions about the sum of all $z(n)$ in the range $[n_0, n_L)$, represented as $S(n_0, n_L)$. First, 
$$S(0, n) = S(0, F_x) + S(F_x, n)$$
where $F_x$ is the largest Fibonacci number lower than $n$. Then, 
$$S(F_x, n) = S(0, n - F_x) + n - F_x$$
because for each $i$ in $[F_x, n)$, $z(i) = z(i - F_x) + 1$. Lastly, 
$$S(0, F_x) = S(0, F_{x-1}) + S(0, F_{x-2}) + F_{x-2}$$
which naturally follows from the previous two, taking into account that $F_x - F_{x-1} = F_{x-2}$.

The $F_i$ and $S(0, F_i)$ values for all $i$ where $F_i \le L$.

Some results in the form  
`<L> <time in ms>: <sum of z(n)>`

<details>
<summary>Results up to L = 10^18</summary>

```
10^1  0 ms: 13
10^2  0 ms: 261
10^3  0 ms: 4003
10^4  0 ms: 52810
10^5  0 ms: 658203
10^6  0 ms: 7894453
10^7  0 ms: 92359637
10^8  0 ms: 1061261095
10^9  0 ms: 11884113961
10^10 0 ms: 131821762778
10^11 0 ms: 1450616011157
10^12 0 ms: 15869246648953
10^13 0 ms: 172050466262882
10^14 0 ms: 1850672620720718
10^15 0 ms: 19805245825361202
10^16 0 ms: 211239038560008696
10^17 0 ms: 2252639041804718029
10^18 0 ms: 5351032935182715336
```
</details>

Using BigInteger we can increase that to:
<details>
<summary>BigInteger results up to L = 10^(2^15)</summary>

```
10^(2^0)  0 ms: 13
10^(2^1)  0 ms: 261
10^(2^2)  0 ms: 52810
10^(2^3)  1 ms: 1061261095
10^(2^4)  1 ms: 211239038560008696
10^(2^5)  2 ms: 4230827727552306957369763484063038
10^(2^6)  5 ms: 846578492368333793332210639208591173901828550662936425498442714457
10^(2^7)  10 ms: 16926491789242879594256143428970933493602610997567793059409268338183760020120796566594414312550138716312342731195462464793346344570
10^(2^8)  18 ms: 3385432309152556501884286271411314365134677416894157768638481962266220143851184845241312180834857073515083564420876608566464943809978566713209952744499973551280533952664955337774148816491402619458572459415709534137978312233351419985723524968625050015924483401
10^(2^9)  9 ms: 
10^(2^10) 21 ms: 
10^(2^11) 37 ms: 
10^(2^12) 99 ms: 
10^(2^13) 328 ms: 
10^(2^14) 1067 ms: 
10^(2^15) 3544 ms: 
```
</details>
